steps.speaker: 旁白
steps.text: Mrs. Henderson's brunch. You finally meet Lisa — warm, loud, full of energy. She takes pictures of everything. You also notice something: Kevin can't stop looking at her.
steps.text_cn: Henderson太太的早午餐。你终于见到了Lisa，她热情、爽朗、充满活力，什么都要拍照。你还注意到一件事：Kevin一直忍不住看她。

steps.instruction: 请把下列英文与对应的中文意思配对。
steps.speech_text: Match these words: dining room, table, kitchen.
steps.feedback: Great! You know the rooms for eating and cooking. / 很好！你知道吃饭和做饭的房间了。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Dining room' = 餐厅, 'table' = 桌子, 'kitchen' = 厨房。

steps.speaker: Mrs. Henderson
steps.text: Lisa, you and Kevin should go to that food festival! You both love Thai food!
steps.text_cn: Lisa，你和Kevin应该一起去那个美食节！你们都喜欢泰国菜！

steps.instruction: "Kevin likes Lisa. Everyone knows." — What does "everyone knows" mean?
steps.feedback: Right! 'Everyone' means all the people. / 对！'Everyone' 意思是所有人。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Everyone knows' = all the people know. It's not a secret! / 'Everyone knows' = 所有人都知道。这不是秘密！

steps.speaker: Jake
steps.text: Kevin likes Lisa. Everyone knows. This is going to be interesting.
steps.text_cn: Kevin喜欢Lisa。大家都知道。这下有意思了。

steps.instruction: Mrs. Henderson invited all the neighbors for brunch at her place. People eat, talk, and laugh together. In the US, is this common?
steps.feedback: Yes! In the US, neighbors often become like family. Brunch together is very normal! / 对！在美国，邻居常常像家人一样。一起吃早午餐很正常！
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! In the US, neighbors often invite each other for meals. It's a way to build community. / 在美国，邻居们经常互相请吃饭。这是建立社区感的方式。
steps.review_word: Yes — neighbors often share meals together

options.label: A
options.text: Yes — neighbors often share meals together

options.label: B
options.text: No — neighbors never talk to each other

options.label: C
options.text: No — only family can eat together

steps.speaker: 旁白
steps.text: After brunch. Kevin pulls you aside in the hall.
steps.text_cn: 早午餐结束后，Kevin把你拉到走廊一边私下说话。

steps.instruction: "Kevin pulls you aside in the hall." — What did Kevin do?
steps.feedback: Yes! 'Pull aside' means he wants to talk to you privately. / 对！'Pull aside' 意思是他想私下跟你说话。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Pull you aside' = 把你拉到一边，想私下说话。'Push' means the opposite! / 'Pull' 和 'push' 是相反的！

options.label: C
options.text: He closed the door on you

steps.speaker: Kevin
steps.text: Was that weird? Am I being weird?
steps.text_cn: 刚才很奇怪吗？我是不是很奇怪？

steps.instruction: Kevin: "Was that weird? Am I being weird?" 你该怎么安慰他？
steps.feedback: Thoughtful advice! 'She needs time' is kind and wise. / 很贴心的建议！'She needs time' 既善良又明智。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! Kevin is nervous about Lisa — 'She needs time' is the best comfort here. / Kevin在紧张关于Lisa的事——'She needs time' 是最好的安慰。

steps.speaker: You
steps.text: I don't know the rules here. But she seems like she needs time.
steps.text_cn: 我不太懂这里的规则。但她看起来需要一点时间。

steps.speaker: 旁白
steps.text: That night, Lisa messages you: "Maybe coffee sometime? Just us."
steps.text_cn: 那天晚上，Lisa给你发消息："改天喝杯咖啡？就我们俩。"

steps.instruction: 用下列词块拼出正确句子（含1个干扰块）。
steps.answer: He pulled me aside to talk.
steps.normalized_answer: he pulled me aside to talk
steps.feedback: Perfect! 'Pull aside' = take someone to talk privately. / 完美！'Pull aside' = 把某人拉到一边私下说话。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! 'Pulled me aside' is correct — 'pushed' means the opposite direction. / 'Pulled me aside' 才对——'pushed' 是相反方向。
steps.review_word: He pulled me aside to talk.

steps.feedback: Clear and gentle! Great advice-giving tone. / 清晰又温和！很好的建议语气。
steps.feedback_wrong: Almost! Try again: She NEEDS TIME. Stress 'needs' and 'time'. / 再试一次：重读NEEDS和TIME。
